verdicts.title: Clone-from-share copies full day-plan text, silently bypassing the owner's "Show day-by-day plans" privacy toggle
verdicts.reasoning: _clone_trip_record SELECTs and INSERTs morning/afternoon/evening/tip unconditionally (trips.py:1384-1425) and neither clone endpoint reads share_show_plans (grep shows it only exists in the share-CREATE path at trips.py:1192-1233), while fetch_share_payload gates plan render on show_plans (public.py:591-621); since the CTA and clone_trip_from_share_token are reachable regardless of the toggle, a recipient of a plans-hidden share gets the full plan text — directly defeating the en.ts privacy claim 'Show day-by-day plans / ... Photos and documents stay private' (en.ts:1684-1685).

verdicts.title: Cloned to-do places keep dangling dayId/timeOfDay pointing at the source trip's days — invisible on every per-day map view (sibling of the just-fixed template bug)
verdicts.reasoning: _clone_trip_attempt copies src['marked_places_json'] verbatim (trips.py:1309) while cloned days get freshly generated ids (trips.py:1393/1414), and the clone path never calls _clean_marked_places (which templates.py:106-107 uses to strip dayId/timeOfDay for exactly this dangling-id reason); todoMarkers.ts:183 skips any dayId-bearing place whose dayId != selectedDayId on per-day views and the chip lookup days.find(d=>d.id===place.dayId) (todoMarkers.ts:143) returns nothing, so cloned to-dos are hidden from the numbered day they point at and can't be re-slotted.

verdicts.title: Clone references the source owner's cover file; the clone's cover 404s when the original owner deletes their trip or changes the cover
verdicts.reasoning: _clone_trip_attempt copies src['cover_url'] verbatim (trips.py:1319) so the clone points at /static/uploads/<owner_id>/<file>; delete_trip snapshots cover_url via collect_trip_upload_paths (helpers.py:78-80) and physically os.remove()s it via delete_upload_files (trips.py:455, helpers.py:155-159) scoped to the OWNER's namespace with no cross-trip reference check, so the owner deleting their trip (or replacing the cover via the unconditional cover_url=excluded.cover_url upsert) leaves the clone's cover a dangling 404.

verdicts.title: Revoked / expired / archived share page still renders the "I want this trip" clone CTA that then fails with a generic error
verdicts.reasoning: The .share-cta block with the clone link (share.html:379-393) is OUTSIDE the {% if days %}/{% if cost %} guards (which close at 377/329) and the 404 render path passes canonical_url (main.py:1100), so /?cloneFromShare=<token> is emitted on the unavailable page; the clone endpoints return 404/410 (trips.py:1548-1553) and attemptPendingClone surfaces only the generic t('errors.cloneFailedFromCollections') on !res.ok (clone-intent.ts:48-51, 68) with no distinct unavailable message.

verdicts.title: Public share page is hardcoded English despite full en/es/fr/pt app support
verdicts.reasoning: share_page (main.py:1072) does no locale resolution and passes no string table; <html lang="en"> is hardcoded (share.html:2) and every visible string is literal English in the template ('Shared by', 'The path', 'Morning/Afternoon/Evening', 'Like this trip?', 'I want this trip', 'Open the app') — a real gap for a public surface, matching the audit's framing.

verdicts.title: Bulk /api/sync lets a non-owner planner write trips.is_archived — the share/clone gate's source of truth — diverging from the owner-only mirror the dedicated archive route enforces
verdicts.reasoning: The /api/sync UPSERT writes is_archived=excluded.is_archived unconditionally (data.py:340) from the payload (data.py:380) gated only by editable_trip_ids, which batch_editable_trip_ids includes role=='planner' non-owners (helpers.py:291); data.py:307-313 pins isPublic for non-owners but nothing pins is_archived, whereas archive_trip/unarchive_trip only mirror trips.is_archived when is_trip_owner (trips.py:491-497, 581-587), and trips.is_archived is the share gate's source of truth (public.py:540) — dormant because the first-party client sends body {} (api.ts:80) but a crafted payload from any authenticated editor still works.
